creator: romain@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hey!

I have to confess I did not tried to update the port for some times sine the project started to "aggressively" stay up-to-date regarding its dependencies (dependabot on GitHub oepn Pull Requests when dependencies have updates, and the file that tells which versions of the dependencies are required are often too new to be used in FreeBSD.

Of course, it is possible to update the dependencies too, but they are owned by numerous people and maybe switching to a deployment using a virtualenv and pip make more sense (it is at least what I started to do).

Can you tell me more on the way you manage Puppetboard.  Should we keep this port and update it at a lower pace than upstream or should we just deprecate installation as a package?

I am currently on vacations, will be able to look at this next week.
